clinic.support_role: Use support donors for trust rails, service hierarchy, contact visibility, and calm medical credibility.

beauty.support_role: Use support donors for airy editorial pacing, appointment CTAs, and treatment/service clarity.

garden_care.support_role: Use support donors for service calendars, planting palettes, garden proof rails, and consultation intake.

construction.support_role: Use support donors for industrial first-fold authority, phone-forward estimating CTAs, service-scope grouping, coverage logic, and project-process clarity. Do not borrow combat-sports aggression, fabricated project metrics, or landscape softness.
section_plan: Hero: name the construction buyer and project type, show a clear estimating/contact path, and include one grounded planning or jobsite proof object.
Scope clarity: group services by project phase such as preconstruction, sitework, buildout, exterior shell, interiors, or closeout; avoid generic three-card filler.
Process timeline: show how a prospect moves from walkthrough and scope review to proposal, schedule, site coordination, and punch-list closeout without inventing dates or guarantees.
Operations proof: use a unique SVG/vector jobsite board, scope matrix, schedule strip, material grid, or coverage map when no project photos are supplied.
Coverage/service area: make local availability and project fit easy to scan without fabricated office counts or named clients.
Conversion band: repeat who the company is right for, make phone/email/contact low-friction, and keep copy grounded to supplied facts only.
visual_direction: Industrial, field-ready, premium, and controlled. The page should feel like a serious construction operator, not a fight gym, legal brochure, garden studio, or generic local-service template.
Use structured grids, blueprint/site-plan cues, material swatches, schedule boards, and hard-working CTA placement. Accent color should punctuate, not flood the page.
If no project images are supplied, use distinct inline SVG/vector proof surfaces for the hero, process, scope, and coverage sections; never fake photo blocks or route screenshots.
claim_guardrails: Construction pages must not invent bonding status, license numbers, safety records, project counts, years in business, dollar volumes, named clients, inspection outcomes, or completion guarantees.
If the brief does not supply proof, use neutral proof structures: project phases, scope definitions, coordination standards, coverage logic, and next-step clarity.
reject_patterns: fight-gym aggression, fake hardhat hero photos, fake project metrics, generic three-card contractor template, luxury real-estate softness, legal-firm stateliness, landscape/garden pacing

landscape.support_role: Use support donors for service-area cues, project proof, and outdoor section rhythm.

law.support_role: Use support donors for authority hierarchy, proof density, and direct intake paths.

mechanical.support_role: Use support donors for emergency CTAs, phone visibility, and service-area proof.

water_service.support_role: Use support donors for phone-forward field-service CTAs, service-area clarity, quote flow polish, and proof-safe maintenance hierarchy; do not import fake licenses, guarantees, towns, reviews, or response times.
claim_guardrails: Water-service pages must not invent license numbers, bonded/insured status, response times, guarantees, service towns, reviews, ratings, years in business, or water-quality performance claims.
reject_patterns: fake license badge, fake service map pins, fabricated response time, generic contractor cards, dark fight-gym styling, beauty/medspa softness

live_commerce.support_role: Use support donors only for conversion mechanics and section sequencing; preserve proof-safe product education and do not import fake reviews, fake discounts, countdown urgency, media logos, or unsupported performance claims.
claim_guardrails: Live-commerce pages must not invent reviews, ratings, units sold, discounts, was/now prices, countdown timers, influencer endorsements, media logos, or temperature/performance claims.
reject_patterns: fake reviews, fake discount stack, false urgency countdown, influencer/media-logo wall, SaaS dashboard, dark fight-gym styling, warm local-service editorial

roofing.support_role: Use support donors for estimate CTAs, proof modules, and service-area coverage.

restoration.support_role: Use support donors for material proof, careful process explanation, and restrained premium pacing.

atelier.support_role: Use support donors for studio pacing, crafted detail, and premium consultation flow.

combat_sports.support_role: Use support donors only for conversion mechanics, schedule clarity, proof density, and intake flow; do not soften the page into kids classes, family karate, or generic fitness.
section_plan: Hero: serious fight-camp positioning, discipline list, application/trial CTA, contact path, and one grounded facility or coaching-method proof object.
Pro-only positioning: state who the gym is for with hard-edged but realistic language; avoid playful kids-class, family karate, wellness spa, or generic fitness SaaS framing.
Disciplines/programs: group MMA, Muay Thai, BJJ/no-gi, wrestling/grappling, striking, and sparring by athlete intent rather than generic class cards.
Coaching methodology: show structured rounds, drilling, sparring standards, recovery/safety boundaries, and progression without inventing famous coaches or fight records.
Fight-camp/process: explain the intake, assessment, weekly training rhythm, and readiness path in concrete steps.

Facility/proof: use user-provided image assets only; if absent, build distinct inline SVG/vector proof surfaces for mats, cage/ring zones, schedule boards, equipment, or coach notes instead of raster placeholders.
Membership/application CTA: restate fit, keep the next step low-friction, and make the CTA readable at mobile width.
visual_direction: Dark, high-contrast, disciplined, fight-camp aesthetic with restrained accent color and angular structure.
Visual rhythm should feel like a serious training facility, not a cheerful academy, kids program, yoga studio, or polished SaaS dashboard.
Use distinct SVG/vector proof surfaces for training method, programs, facility, and application flow when the user provides no images; no repeated fake icons or SVG blob visuals.
claim_guardrails: Combat-sports pages must not invent pro records, famous fighters, belt lineages, tournament wins, UFC/Bellator/PFL/ONE affiliations, or coach credentials.
If a pro-fighter or fight-camp angle is requested but credentials are not supplied, frame it as training intent, standards, and application fit rather than proven league status.
reject_patterns: family karate, kids-class, after-school, playful emoji, generic fitness club, wellness spa, templated three-card local-service hero, fake league badges, fake champion claims

saas_dashboard.support_role: Use support donors for dense table rhythm, filter placement, status chips, and queue ergonomics — not marketing hero patterns.
section_plan: App shell: sticky top bar with product context, global search, primary actions, and user menu.
Summary strip: KPI/status cards for queue depth, SLA risk, and assignment load using neutral tokens.
Filter toolbar: saved views, facet chips, date range, owner filter, and bulk actions.
Primary table: sortable triage queue with status chips, owner, updated time, and row actions.
Side detail / drawer pattern: selected row context without leaving the queue.
State panels: visible loading skeleton, empty queue guidance, and error retry block.
Footer/help: compact support link and keyboard shortcut hints.
visual_direction: Dense, neutral B2B ops UI with tight spacing, table-first hierarchy, and restrained accent color.
Avoid marketing landing-page hero framing, fight-gym darkness, or local-service phone-pill patterns.
claim_guardrails: Do not invent customer counts, uptime percentages, or client logos.
Status summaries must use neutral placeholder labels unless real data is supplied.

academy.support_role: Use support donors for class/program hierarchy, schedule clarity, and intake CTAs.

aerospace_launch.support_role: Use support examples for dark technical sequencing, specification rhythm, and manifest/intake flow only.

fintech_terminal.support_role: Use support examples for dense terminal rhythm, status strips, chart/table composition, and product-console proof.

developer_docs.support_role: Use support examples for docs shell, code block rhythm, page outline, callouts, and footer taxonomy.

luxury_maison.support_role: Use support examples for serif hierarchy, slow editorial rhythm, restrained CTAs, newsletter and boutique footer composition.

reference_product.support_role: Use support examples for premium object focus, materials/proof sequencing, measurement tables, and compact reserve modules.

interactive_instrument.support_role: Use support examples for minimal interactive stage composition and focused control hierarchy, not for marketing-page sections.

auto_detailing.support_role: Use support donors for service grids, phone-priority CTAs, process steps, and local proof — never beauty or medspa donors.

rhythm_breaks: section 2 must break from the dark hero with a method or program composition that is not cards
section 5 must become a training-board, schedule-sheet, or editorial reset before the final CTA
combat_sports.surface_palette_distribution: 70 percent disciplined dark canvas, 20 percent training-board contrast, 10 percent red accent as punctuation only
combat_sports.density_curve: compressed first fold, methodical middle, quiet proof reset, direct intake close

local_model_failure_patterns.if: the Programs section becomes a row of equal cards
local_model_failure_patterns.then: replace it with a numbered training-path list plus one inset artifact that shows progression

local_model_failure_patterns.if: two SVG vocabulary blocks share the same rectangle and thin-line grammar
local_model_failure_patterns.then: make the next visual a radial, editorial, dimensional, or spatial artifact instead

local_model_failure_patterns.if: the hero echoes the donor outline-word or fight poster motif
local_model_failure_patterns.then: remove it and use an assessment, mat-zone, or schedule artifact tied to the brief

local_model_failure_patterns.if: the footer copies the footer_full atom identity
local_model_failure_patterns.then: keep contact mechanics only and rebuild the layout labels from scratch

local_model_failure_patterns.if: every scroll reveal uses one uniform stagger
local_model_failure_patterns.then: reduce motion and reserve a single path-draw or hover state for the signature artifact

local_model_failure_patterns.if: the whole page stays dark plate after dark plate
local_model_failure_patterns.then: insert one controlled tonal break such as a whiteboard, schedule sheet, or editorial method band

local_model_failure_patterns.if: copy drifts into champion, league, belt, or coach authority claims
local_model_failure_patterns.then: rewrite proof as training structure, readiness, and intake fit only

rhythm_breaks: section 2 must move from the hero into a diagnostic selector or system readout, not a row of generic service cards
one mid-page section must interrupt service copy with an equipment diagram, service-area shell, or emergency-vs-planned split
water_service.surface_palette_distribution: 55 percent light field-service canvas, 30 percent dark diagnostic panels, 15 percent blue/cyan accent as functional signal
water_service.density_curve: diagnostic first fold, technical education middle, proof-safe service-area and quote close
forbidden_repetitions: no fake license or guarantee badges
no fabricated towns, pins, ratings, or response times

rhythm_breaks: section 2 must become a live demo sequence or product schematic, not generic ecommerce cards
one mid-page section must switch from broadcast cadence to offer matrix, objection FAQ, or platform checklist
live_commerce.surface_palette_distribution: 60 percent dark broadcast canvas, 25 percent icy product panels, 15 percent crisp blue accent as live/interactive signal
live_commerce.density_curve: broadcast first fold, product education middle, shoppable comparison and objection close
forbidden_repetitions: no fake reviews or units-sold counters
no fabricated discount math or countdown urgency
no repeated video-card rail without a product education job

rhythm_breaks: section 1 must be app shell + summary strip, not a marketing hero
one mid-page section must switch from table density to state panel or drawer detail
saas_dashboard.surface_palette_distribution: 70 percent neutral canvas, 20 percent elevated panels, 10 percent status accent chips
saas_dashboard.density_curve: dense summary first, table-heavy middle, compact utility footer
forbidden_repetitions: no marketing hero above the fold
no repeated generic SVG filler across table rows

rhythm_breaks: section 2 must change layout shape from the hero and introduce either editorial type or a proof surface
one mid-page section must interrupt repeated cards with an asymmetric proof, process, or intake composition
clinic.surface_palette_distribution: 60 percent base canvas, 25 percent elevated surfaces, 15 percent contrast or editorial break, accent color as punctuation only
clinic.density_curve: dense first fold to clear service body to focused conversion close
forbidden_repetitions: no repeated SVG vocabulary across adjacent sections

local_model_failure_patterns.if: the clinic page becomes a row of equal cards
local_model_failure_patterns.then: replace one card row with an asymmetric method, proof, or intake module

local_model_failure_patterns.if: two SVG blocks use the same rectangles and thin strokes
local_model_failure_patterns.then: rebuild the later block with a different vocabulary from Visual Artifact Specs

local_model_failure_patterns.if: the hero repeats a decorative motif from the anchor excerpt
local_model_failure_patterns.then: swap it for a route-specific proof surface or editorial device

local_model_failure_patterns.if: the footer mirrors the atom shape verbatim
local_model_failure_patterns.then: keep the contact mechanics but rebuild the grid and labels for the brief

local_model_failure_patterns.if: every reveal animation has identical timing
local_model_failure_patterns.then: use motion only where it clarifies hierarchy and honor reduced motion

local_model_failure_patterns.if: the palette stays in one tonal register for the full page
local_model_failure_patterns.then: insert a deliberate contrast band or calmer section to reset rhythm

rhythm_breaks: hero must establish the technical object and mission context before proof or form sections
one mid-page section must switch from narrative editorial copy to specs/table/readout density
aerospace_launch.surface_palette_distribution: dark technical canvas with sparse warm/high-energy accents and restrained panels
aerospace_launch.density_curve: cinematic hero to technical body to manifest/intake close

rhythm_breaks: hero must expose a readable product console or data surface
one section must carry table/chart density rather than generic feature cards
fintech_terminal.surface_palette_distribution: dark neutral shell, blue command accents, status colors only for data meaning
fintech_terminal.density_curve: product console first, dense capability body, compact CTA close

rhythm_breaks: the page is a docs article, not a marketing landing page
code blocks and callouts must be part of the reading flow
developer_docs.surface_palette_distribution: dark docs canvas with restrained semantic accent and readable code surfaces
developer_docs.density_curve: nav shell to step-by-step article to footer taxonomy

rhythm_breaks: use a slow editorial principle section before conversion
include one deliberate contrast band or quote-like reset
luxury_maison.surface_palette_distribution: near-black editorial canvas, cream contrast band, warm serif foreground
luxury_maison.density_curve: spacious literary hero to numbered principles to restrained CTA/footer

rhythm_breaks: hero must center the product object and reserve conversion path
specs must appear with measurement context or placeholders
reference_product.surface_palette_distribution: black product theater with warm metal accents and proof/spec panels
reference_product.density_curve: object hero to thesis to materials/proof to reserve close

rhythm_breaks: first screen is the working instrument or honest preview, not a feature list
instructions remain microcopy around the control surface
interactive_instrument.surface_palette_distribution: minimal black studio field with one or two signal accents
interactive_instrument.density_curve: single centered control with optional compact secondary surfaces

one mid-page section must interrupt repeated cards with an asymmetric proof, process, or intake composition

auto_detailing.density_curve: dense first fold to clear service body to focused conversion close

no waxing or medspa treatment-menu cards

local_model_failure_patterns.if: the detailing page becomes a beauty or spa editorial
